title: Cheese on toast
subtitle: Thick toasted bread topped with mature cheddar and mustard under the grill.
excerpt: Thick slices of bread toasted on one side, spread with a little mustard, piled with grated mature cheddar, and grilled until bubbling and beginning to blister. Ready in five minutes. The British answer to the croque-monsieur.
sourceNotes: Cheese on toast is one of the oldest recorded British snack preparations, appearing in cookery books from the eighteenth century as a savoury course served after the main meal. Welsh rarebit, its more elaborate sibling, adds a cooked cheese sauce poured over the toast; plain cheese on toast is the simpler, faster version that became a staple of the British home kitchen. The mustard is essential: it cuts the richness of the cheese and prevents the dish from being merely bland.

content.text: Toast one side only: the untoasted side goes under the grill with the cheese, so it gets toasted from the grill heat while the cheese melts and browns on top. This produces a crisp base and melted top without the bread drying out from double toasting.
Use mature cheddar for flavour. Mild cheddar melts but tastes of very little. Grating rather than slicing gives more even coverage and better browning.
A grill or oven grill element. A baking tray to hold the bread.
Heat the grill to high. Place the bread on a baking tray and toast under the grill on one side only until golden. Remove from the grill.

Turn the bread over. Spread the untoasted side with a thin scrape of {{dijon-mustard}} of mustard. Top generously with {{cheddar}} of grated cheddar. Add a few drops of Worcestershire sauce and a pinch of black pepper if using. Return to the grill for 2 to 3 minutes until the cheese is melted, bubbling, and beginning to brown in patches. Serve immediately.

items.fix: Move the tray to the highest shelf position, as close to the grill element as possible.
items.cause: Grill not close enough to the element.
items.symptom: Cheese is melted but not browned

items.fix: Toast the first side properly so the base is firm. Drier mature cheddar releases less liquid when melting.
items.cause: Too much moisture in the cheese, or bread was not toasted on the first side.

items.fix: For thick bread, toast the first side longer before adding cheese, so the bread timing and cheese timing align.
items.cause: Grill too hot, or bread too far from the element.
items.symptom: Cheese is burning on top before the bread is ready

items.fix: Use the most mature cheddar available. The mustard is not decoration. It is structural to the flavour.

content.text: Cheese on toast is one of the few British dishes that has remained almost entirely unchanged for several centuries. It was served as a savoury course at formal dinners in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, a tradition that persisted in Oxford and Cambridge colleges well into the twentieth century. In everyday British cooking it occupies the position of comfort food made from what is always available: bread, hard cheese, and a grill. The Welsh rarebit tradition adds a sauce; cheese on toast dispenses with the sauce and is faster for it.
